title: Blakea cuprina

The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Blakea cuprina include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
